topic.name: Tax Credits and Deductions
topic.description: This cluster focuses on various amendments to the Internal Revenue Code related to tax credits and deductions for energy investments, caregiving, and education.

Document 1: amends the Internal Revenue Code to extend through 2018 the energy tax credit for investment in qualified microturbine property.
Document 2: amends the Internal Revenue Code to extend through 2018 the energy tax credit for investment in thermal energy property.
Document 3: Amends the Internal Revenue Code to allow caregivers of family members with long-term care needs a refundable tax credit of $3,000.
Document 4: Amends the Internal Revenue Code to allow all taxpayers a tax deduction for expenses relating to the home schooling of their children.
Document 5: Amends the Internal Revenue Code to allow an energy tax credit for investment in equipment that uses natural gas to heat and cool a structure.
Document 6: amends the Internal Revenue Code to extend through 2018 the tax credit for qualified fuel cell property.
Document 7: Amends the Internal Revenue Code to extend through 2015 the tax deduction for mortgage insurance premiums.

topic.name: Healthcare Coverage Legislation
topic.description: Legislation aimed at expanding healthcare coverage for specific medical conditions and services under Medicare and Medicaid.

Document 1: Amends title XVIII (Medicare) of the Social Security Act to extend coverage to lymphedema diagnosis and treatment services.
Lymphedema Diagnosis and Treatment Cost Savings Act of 2011 - extends coverage to lymphedema diagnosis and treatment services.
Document 2: Amends title XVIII (Medicare) of the Social Security Act to cover pharmacist services.
Document 3: Medicaid Obesity Treatment Act of 2007 - Amends title XIX (Medicaid) of the Social Security Act to require Medicaid drug coverage of drugs prescribed for treatment of obesity.
Document 4: Beneficiary Respiratory Equipment Access and Transparency to Home Ventilator Care Act of 2016 amends title XVIII (Medicare) of the Social Security Act to require the Centers for Medicare & Medicaid Services to establish policies and standards for determining the appropriate use of certain ventilators under the Medicare program.
Document 6: Pharmacy and Medically Underserved Areas Enhancement Act amends title XVIII (Medicare) of the Social Security Act to cover pharmacist services.

topic.name: Education Funding and Support
topic.description: The documents focus on legislative acts aimed at enhancing financial literacy, supporting teachers, and improving educational resources and services in schools.

Document 1: amends title IV (Student Assistance) of the Higher Education Act of 1965
consumer-tested online counseling tool for first-time federal student loan borrowers.
Document 2: requires institutions of higher education to use a portion of funding to compensate students
give priority to public elementary or secondary schools serving low-income communities.
Document 3: amends title IV (Student Assistance) of the Higher Education Act of 1965
allows an eligible teacher to use the same teaching service to qualify for both the teacher and public service loan forgiveness programs.
Document 4: provides student loan forgiveness under the Federal Family Education Loan and Direct loan programs
teachers of students of limited English proficiency.
Document 5: directs the Administrator of the Environmental Protection Agency to review, update, revise, and publish the Radon Measurements In Schools Guidelines
awarding grants to states to test radon levels in public elementary and secondary schools.
Document 6: authorize the Secretary of Education to award matching grants to states
integrate financial literacy education into public elementary and secondary schools.
Document 7: authorize the Secretary of Education to award matching grants to states
train secondary school financial literacy or entrepreneurship teachers.

topic.name: Temporary Tariff Suspensions
topic.description: The documents collectively address amendments to the Harmonized Tariff Schedule of the United States concerning temporary suspensions or modifications of duties on various products.

Document 1: suspend temporarily the duty on certain acrylic staple fibers.
Document 2: modify the article description of certain low expansion laboratory glassware
extend the temporary reduction of duty on such glassware.
Document 3: suspend temporarily the duty on certain synthetic staple fibers that are not carded, combed, or otherwise processed for spinning.
Document 4: suspend temporarily the duty on a certain chemical that is used for dyeing apparel home textiles.
Document 5: extend the temporary suspension of duty on certain 6-volt batteries.
Document 6: extend the temporary suspension of duty on yarn of carded camel hair.
Document 7: extend the temporary suspension of duty on self contained, carafe-less automatic drip coffeemaker with electronic clock.

topic.name: Veterans Health Care and Benefits
topic.description: Legislation and initiatives aimed at improving health care services and benefits for veterans.

Document 1: establish a program on training and certification of family caregivers of veterans
conduct outreach to enhance awareness of veterans and the public about the symptoms of post-traumatic stress disorder (PTSD) and TBI
Document 2: require the U.S. Postal Service to issue and sell a Veterans Health Care Stamp to help fund the medical care and treatment of veterans
Document 3: provide members of the Armed Forces and veterans with individualized information concerning veterans' benefits
notify the member or veteran of such benefits
Document 4: authorize the city of Aurora, Colorado, to convey land for construction of a veterans' medical facility
Document 5: provide an effective date of an award of disability compensation for veterans
Document 6: authorize the Secretary of Veterans Affairs to reimburse health professionals for continuing professional education expenses
Document 7: allow any veteran with a service-connected disability to receive hospital care or medical services at any eligible facility

topic.name: U.S. Energy Policy and Regulation
topic.description: This cluster addresses various legislative acts related to the exploration, production, and regulation of oil and natural gas resources in the United States, particularly focusing on Alaska and the Outer Continental Shelf.

Document 1: Amends the Alaska Natural Gas Pipeline Act of 2004
Alaska natural gas to the Canadian border
Document 2: Prohibits any federal agency or official from imposing a fee or tax on gaseous emissions
greenhouse gas emissions on climate change
Document 3: the National Petroleum Reserve (NPR) in Alaska remains explicitly designated to provide oil and natural gas resources
require the mandatory program of competitive leasing of oil and gas in the NPR
Document 4: direct the Secretary of Energy to establish a fee for any nonproducing oil or gas leases
Outer Continental Shelf Lands Act
Document 5: Prohibits the Secretary of the Treasury and the Administrator of the Environmental Protection Agency from devising or implementing a carbon tax
tax, fee, or price on emissions
Document 6: establish the American-Made Energy Trust Fund for alternative and renewable energy incentives
oil and gas leasing program that will result in an environmentally sound program
Document 7: declare that any royalty under an oil and gas lease on submerged lands
increase the minimum royalty under such leases from 12.5% to 20%

topic.name: U.S. Immigration Policy and Enforcement
topic.description: This cluster focuses on various legislative amendments and acts related to immigration policy, enforcement, and the treatment of immigrants in the United States.

Document 1: prohibit issuance of a nonimmigrant temporary religious worker visa to an alien whose country of nationality has been determined by the Secretary of Homeland Security to not extend reciprocal immigration treatment to U.S. nationals.
Document 2: allow employers to use the employment eligibility confirmation system to verify the identity and employment eligibility of an employee from the time of application for employment.
Document 3: directs the Department of Homeland Security to establish a law enforcement notification system for every state.
Document 4: prohibits the U.S. refugee admission of an alien who is a national of, has habitually resided in, or is claiming refugee status due to events in any country containing terrorist-controlled territory.
Document 5: an alien who is 18 or older, knowingly lacks lawful immigration status, and is present in the United States shall be fined and/or imprisoned.
Document 6: provides increased protections and eligibility for family-sponsored immigrants.
Document 7: establish an employment-based, conditional immigrant visa for a sponsored alien entrepreneur.

topic.name: Electromagnetic Pulse Weapons
topic.description: Legislation concerning the assessment and reporting of foreign countries' electromagnetic pulse weapons programs.

Document 1: Requires the Director of National Intelligence to report to the congressional intelligence and defense committees on the research, development, testing, and deployment programs of foreign countries relating to electromagnetic pulse (EMP) weapons.
Directs that such report identify each country pursuing an EMP weapons program and describe the scope of each such program.
Document 2: Requires the Director of National Intelligence to report to the congressional intelligence and defense committees on the research, development, testing, and deployment programs of foreign countries relating to electromagnetic pulse (EMP) weapons.
Directs that such report identify each country pursuing an EMP weapons program and describe the scope of each such program.

topic.name: Wilderness Area Designation and Management
topic.description: The documents discuss the designation, management, and protection of various wilderness areas and national monuments in the United States, focusing on land administered by the Bureau of Land Management and the National Wilderness Preservation System.

Document 1: Establishes the Browns Canyon National Monument in Colorado to preserve the nationally significant biological, cultural, recreational, natural, wilderness, historic, scenic, riparian, geological, educational, and scientific values found within the National Monument.
Requires the Secretary of the Interior to complete a comprehensive management plan for the conservation and protection of the National Monument and the Wilderness.
Document 2: Directs the Secretary of the Interior to modify the contract to add three years to such contract's expiration date.
Prohibits anything in this Act from affecting any claim by the United States against any timber purchaser.
Document 3: Designates certain Bureau of Land Management (BLM) land in Oregon as wilderness and as a component of the National Wilderness Preservation System.
Permits established livestock grazing in the Wilderness to continue.
Document 4: Designates specified wilderness areas in New Mexico and administered by the Bureau of Land Management (BLM) as wilderness and as components of the National Wilderness Preservation System.
Requires the development of separate management plans for such areas.
Document 5: Designates certain lands in Rocky Mountain National Park, Colorado, as the Rocky Mountain National Park Wilderness and as a component of the National Wilderness Preservation System.
Directs the Secretary of the Interior to establish an alignment line and identify the boundaries for the East Shore Trail.
Document 7: Designates specified federal land managed by the Bureau of Land Management (BLM) in Humboldt County, Nevada, as the Pine Forest Range Wilderness.
Authorizes the Secretary of the Interior to take necessary measures to control fire, insects, and diseases in the wilderness designated by this Act.

topic.name: Child Exploitation and Trafficking
topic.description: Legislation aimed at addressing and preventing child exploitation, trafficking, and abuse through enhanced legal frameworks and support systems.

Document 1: require funds from the annual grant to be used to provide technical assistance to law enforcement agencies
identifying, locating, and recovering victims of child sex trafficking
Document 2: expand the jurisdictional basis for prosecutions of sexual exploitation of children
include child pornography that contains a visual depiction of an actual minor engaging in sexually explicit conduct
Document 3: develop and publish guidelines to assist state child welfare agencies in efforts to serve youth who are victims of trafficking
require a state plan for foster care to identify and document victims of trafficking
Document 4: require the Office of Juvenile Justice and Delinquency Prevention's annual grant to operate a cyber tipline
report missing children who are identified as victims of trafficking
Document 5: give Indian tribes criminal jurisdiction over domestic violence and violations of protective orders
provide grants to assist Indian tribes in securing the rights of victims
Document 6: include sex trafficking as a target of the grants to Indian tribal governments
address the needs of youth who are victims of domestic violence, sexual assault, or sex trafficking
Document 7: initial mental health screening of any child in foster care must be completed within 30 days
comprehensive assessment of the child's mental health must be completed within 60 days

topic.name: Mental Health Services Legislation
topic.description: This cluster focuses on various legislative acts aimed at improving mental health services and support for specific populations, including veterans, children, and college students.

Document 1: Veteran Urgent Access to Mental Healthcare Act
provide former members of the Armed Forces with an initial mental health assessment
Document 2: Mental Health in Schools Act of 2015
assist local communities and schools in applying a public health approach to mental health services
Document 3: Mental Health on Campus Improvement Act
award grants to eligible institutions of higher education to improve mental and behavioral health services
Document 4: Mental Health on Campus Improvement Act
improve mental and behavioral health services and outreach on college and university campuses
Document 5: Community Assistance Act for Persons with Mental Illness
provide housing counseling and financial counseling for individuals before their release from institutions
Document 6: National Autism Spectrum Disorders Initiative Act of 2011
improve the lives of persons with autism spectrum disorders through research focused on prevention
Document 7: Combating Autism Reauthorization Act
reauthorizes appropriations for programs established under the Combating Autism Act of 2006

topic.name: Drug Regulation and Safety
topic.description: This topic encompasses legislation related to the regulation, safety, and distribution of drugs and controlled substances, focusing on their effects on public health and the environment.

Document 1: Amends the Controlled Substances Act to impose additional penalties for drug offenses on federal property.
Prohibits harmful practices related to drug cultivation on federal land.
Document 2: Establishes the Center for Postmarket Evaluation and Research for Drugs and Biologics within the FDA.
Requires safety assessments and public information on FDA-approved drugs.
Document 3: Appropriates funding for activities related to opioid abuse and treatment services.
Amends the Controlled Substances Act to allow partial filling of opioid prescriptions.
Document 4: Prohibits the manufacture of generic drugs until the exclusivity period expires.
Document 5: Delays drug approval until DOJ issues a final rule for controlled substances.
Establishes timelines for drug product registration.
Document 6: Allows compounding of drugs for special needs patients under certain regulations.
Document 7: Includes postmarket safety activities in the review process for human drug applications.

topic.name: Ecosystem Restoration and Water Management
topic.description: The documents focus on legislative acts aimed at ecosystem restoration, water rights, and water management projects across various regions in the United States.

Document 1: Authorizes the Secretary of the Army to carry out the project for ecosystem restoration, water supply, flood control, and protection of water quality.
Deauthorizes certain projects authorized under the Water Resources Development Act of 2000.
Document 2: Authorizes, ratifies, and confirms the Big Sandy River-Planet Ranch Water Rights Settlement Agreement.
Directs the Secretary of Interior to execute both agreements.
Document 3: Directs the Secretary of the Interior, in coordination with Los Angeles County, California, to prepare a feasibility study for environmental ecosystem restoration.
Identifies demonstration projects for construction and restoration of the San Gabriel River Watershed.
Document 4: Amends the Reclamation Wastewater and Groundwater Study and Facilities Act to authorize the Secretary of the Interior to participate in the planning, design, and construction of water recycling facilities.
Enhance and restore riparian habitat in the Black Wash Sonoran Desert ecosystem.
Document 5: Directs the Chief of Engineers, in coordination with Los Angeles County, California, to prepare a feasibility study for environmental ecosystem restoration.
Limits to 65% the federal share of the cost of any project under this Act.
Document 6: Amends the Reclamation Wastewater and Groundwater Study and Facilities Act to authorize the Secretary of the Interior, in cooperation with the city of San Juan Capistrano, California, to participate in the design, planning, and construction of an advanced water treatment plant facility.
Participate in the design, planning, and construction of a project to expand reclaimed water distribution, storage, and treatment facilities.
Document 7: Authorizes the Secretary of the Army to carry out the project for ecosystem restoration, water supply, flood control, and protection of water quality.
Authorizes the Secretary of the Army to carry out the project for ecosystem restoration, Picayune Strand, Collier County, Florida.

topic.name: Housing and Foreclosure Assistance
topic.description: Legislation focused on providing assistance and modifications to homeowners facing foreclosure and promoting affordable housing solutions.

Document 1: Aiding Those Facing Foreclosure Act of 2010
legal assistance to homeowners of owner-occupied homes whose mortgages are in default or delinquency.
Document 2: Workforce Housing Opportunity Simplification Act of 2010
authorize GSEs to purchase affordable workforce housing mortgages.
Document 3: Home Foreclosure Reduction Act of 2015
amends federal bankruptcy law governing Chapter 13 debtors regarding foreclosure.
Document 4: Preserving Homes and Communities Act of 2011
prohibits mortgagees from initiating foreclosure without exploring loan modification options.
Document 5: Stopping Ongoing Lender Delays Act
requires mortgage servicers to respond to short sale requests within 30 days.
Document 6: amends the Truth in Lending Act
modifies the definition of mortgage originator.
Document 7: Housing Opportunity and Mortgage Equity Act of 2010
refinancing of qualified single-family housing mortgages.

topic.name: Coral Reef Conservation Legislation
topic.description: Legislation focused on the conservation, restoration, and management of coral reef ecosystems in the United States.

Document 1: amends the Coral Reef Conservation Act of 2000
authorize emergency actions to minimize injury to a coral reef
Document 2: direct the Secretary of Commerce to submit to specified congressional committees a national coral reef ecosystem action strategy
make grants for projects for the conservation of coral reef ecosystems
Document 3: authorize the 12 federal agencies on the U.S. Coral Reef Task Force to carry out prize competitions that promote coral reef research
establish programs that address communities, environments, or industries that are in distress due to the decline or degradation of coral reef ecosystems
Document 4: extend the award of remaining coral reef conservation program grant funds
authorize actions to minimize injury to a coral reef or loss of an ecosystem function
Document 5: amends the Coral Reef Conservation Act of 2000 to extend the award of remaining coral reef conservation program grant funds
authorize the Administrator of the National Oceanic and Atmospheric Administration (NOAA) to undertake or authorize actions to minimize injury to a coral reef
Document 6: direct the Secretary of Commerce to make grants for projects for the conservation of coral reef ecosystems
authorize the Secretary to conduct activities with local, regional, or international programs and partners
Document 7: amends the Coral Reef Conservation Act of 2000 to transfer certain authority from the Administrator of NOAA to the Secretary of Commerce
direct the Secretary to submit to Congress a national coral reef ecosystem action strategy

topic.name: Agricultural Policy and Support Programs
topic.description: This cluster focuses on legislative acts related to agricultural policies, support programs for farmers, and specific provisions for dairy products and veterans in agriculture.

Document 1: Amends the Agricultural Marketing Act of 1946 to include dairy products as a covered commodity.
Requires the retailer of a covered commodity that is a dairy product to designate such commodity's country of origin.
Document 2: Amends the Federal Crop Insurance Act to reduce crop insurance assistance for crops grown on native sod acreage converted to cropland.
Requires offering farmers supplemental crop insurance based on an area yield and loss plan of insurance.
Document 4: Directs the Secretary of Agriculture (USDA) to establish a dairy producer margin protection program.
Establishes a dairy market stabilization program for all U.S. dairy producers.

topic.name: Renewable Energy Legislation
topic.description: This cluster of documents focuses on various legislative efforts aimed at promoting renewable energy sources and reducing reliance on fossil fuels in the United States.

Document 1: Amends the Clean Air Act to revise the renewable fuel program
Defines 'next generation biofuel' to mean renewable fuel derived from renewable biomass.
Document 2: Prohibits the EPA from proposing standards for carbon dioxide emissions from existing fossil fuel-fired electric utility generating units
Focuses on standards of performance under the Clean Air Act.
Document 3: Sets forth a federal renewable energy portfolio standard
Instructs the Secretary of Energy to establish a renewable energy credit trading program.
Document 4: Requires a retail electric supplier to submit renewable energy credits to the Department of Energy
Prescribes a schedule of graduated annual percentages of renewable energy generation.
Document 5: Amends the Clean Air Act to regulate the volume of ethanol in renewable fuel
Imposes penalties for failures to comply with advanced biofuel standards.
Document 6: Extends the income tax credit for biodiesel and renewable diesel used as fuel.
Document 7: Directs the Secretary of Energy to conduct research on technologies to reduce petroleum use and emissions from vehicles
Includes activities in hybridization and electrification of vehicle systems.

topic.name: Data Security and Breach Notification
topic.description: This topic encompasses legislation aimed at enhancing the security of personal information and establishing protocols for breach notifications across various sectors, including healthcare and education.

Document 1: One Hour Notification Act of 2013 - Directs the Secretary of Health and Human Services to notify individuals within one hour after a breach of personal information.
Requires annual reporting to Congress on breaches and security measures.
Document 2: Amends FERPA to require educational institutions to establish information security practices and breach notification policies.
Expands the definition of education records to include digital formats and outsourced services.
Document 3: Requires the Government Publishing Office to provide public access to congressionally mandated reports while ensuring sensitive information is redacted.
Agencies must consider partial disclosure of information when full disclosure is not possible.
Document 4: Requires entities to take reasonable measures to protect personal information and notify individuals of breaches that may cause harm.
Sets forth civil monetary penalties for violations of data security provisions.
Document 5: Requires the Department of Health and Human Services to notify individuals of breaches involving their personal information within two business days.
Focuses on security of systems maintained by health care exchanges.
Document 6: Directs covered entities to notify individuals of breaches that may cause identity theft or financial harm.
Establishes methods for notification and allows delays under certain circumstances.
Document 7: Amends federal law to impose criminal penalties for failures to notify of security breaches involving sensitive personal information.
Defines major security breaches and outlines notification requirements to federal authorities.

topic.name: Tax Fraud Prevention and Regulation
topic.description: Legislation aimed at preventing tax fraud, protecting taxpayer information, and regulating tax preparation practices.

Document 1: authorize the Secretary of the Treasury to disclose tax return information to federal and state law enforcement agencies
implement an identity theft tax fraud prevention program
Document 2: directs the Department of the Treasury to develop referral procedures for tax preparation firms
develop comprehensive guidance for the treatment by IRS of all claims for refunds relating to tax return preparer fraud
Document 3: issue uniform standards for the method for truncation of Social Security account numbers
prohibit the display of Social Security account numbers to the general public on the Internet
Document 4: establish a program to license or certify and regulate tax return preparers
require that the tax return preparer demonstrate good character, good reputation, necessary qualifications
Document 5: prohibits the payment of any performance award to an employee of the Internal Revenue Service (IRS) who owes an outstanding federal tax debt
Document 6: increase civil penalties for willful violations of anti-money laundering laws
impose personal liability on officers, directors, employees, or affiliated parties of a financial institution for fines
Document 7: authorize the Secretary of the Treasury to disclose tax return information to federal and state law enforcement agencies

review whether current tax laws and regulations related to the confidentiality and disclosure of tax return information prevent the effective enforcement of federal, state, and local identity theft statutes

topic.name: Financial Regulatory Relief
topic.description: Documents focus on legislative acts aimed at providing regulatory relief and adjustments for financial institutions, particularly smaller banks and depository institutions.

Document 1: Amends the Sarbanes-Oxley Act of 2002 to exempt from its rules regarding management assessment of internal controls the following institutions which, as of the end of the preceding fiscal year, had total consolidated assets of $1 billion or less.

Amends the Truth in Lending Act (TILA) to require the Consumer Financial Protection Bureau (CFPB) to exempt from requirements governing escrow or impound accounts affecting certain consumer credit transactions.
Document 2: Amends the Securities Exchange Act of 1934 to require an issuer that is a savings and loan holding company to register with the Securities and Exchange Commission (SEC) if its assets exceed $10 million.

Suspends automatically the duty of a savings and loan holding company to file supplementary and periodic information if the securities of each class to which the registration statement relates are held of record by fewer than 1,200 persons.
Document 3: This bill directs the Board of Governors of the Federal Reserve System to increase the asset threshold under the Small Bank Holding Company Policy Statement.

The bill also exempts from the internal control attestation requirements of the Sarbanes-Oxley Act of 2002 both an insured depository institution and a depository institution holding company with consolidated assets of less than $1 billion.
Document 4: This bill amends the Dodd-Frank Wall Street Reform and Consumer Protection Act to authorize the Financial Stability Oversight Council (FSOC) to subject a bank holding company to enhanced supervision.
FSOC's determination must be based upon specified factors, using an indicator-based measurement approach established by the Basel Committee on Banking Supervision.
Document 5: Amends the Sarbanes-Oxley Act of 2002 to require rules of the Securities and Exchange Commission (SEC) to permit an insured depository institution, a bank holding company, or a savings and loan holding company to elect voluntarily not to provide otherwise mandatory certifications.
Document 6: Amends the Federal Deposit Insurance Act to repeal requirements for least-cost resolution of troubled insured depository institutions.
Amends the Federal Reserve Act to repeal limitations on advances to member banks by the Board of Governors of the Federal Reserve System.
Document 7: Amends the Financial Stability Act of 2010 to subject the Financial Stability Oversight Council (Council) to the Administrative Procedure Act.
Requires the Council to make publicly available any final report study or analysis it has prepared, unless exempt from disclosure.

topic.name: Wildlife Conservation Legislation
topic.description: Legislation aimed at the conservation of wildlife habitats and species, particularly focusing on migratory birds and endangered species.

Document 1: Amends the Wetlands Loan Act to increase and extend funding for conservation of migratory waterfowl and habitat essential to the preservation of such waterfowl
funds provided pursuant to this Act should be used for preserving and increasing waterfowl populations
Document 2: Requires the Secretary of the Treasury to establish a program to provide loans and loan guarantees to enable state political subdivisions to acquire interests in real property pursuant to habitat conservation plans
give preference to such subdivisions located in biologically rich regions in which rapid growth and development threaten successful implementation of approved habitat conservation plans
Document 3: Requires the Administrator of each of the Bonneville Power Administration, the Western Area Power Administration, the Southwestern Power Administration, and the Southeastern Power Administration to include in monthly billing statements estimates of each customer's share of such Administration's direct and indirect costs for compliance with the Endangered Species Act
submit an annual report estimating such costs
Document 4: Removes the Department of the Interior's listing of the lesser prairie chicken as threatened under the Endangered Species Act
requires Interior to monitor and submit an annual report on progress in the conservation of the lesser prairie chicken
Document 5: amends the Migratory Bird Treaty Act to revise standards for determining what constitutes baiting for purposes of the prohibition on taking migratory game birds
Department of Agriculture must submit to the Department of the Interior a report on changes to normal agricultural practices
Document 6: requires an agency when developing or considering approval of a management plan for federal land to ensure that hunting activities are allowed

requires fees charged related to hunting activities on federal land to be limited to what the Secretary of the Interior or the Secretary of Agriculture reasonably estimates to be necessary to offset the costs directly related to management of hunting on such land

topic.name: Legislative Acts for Federal Designations and Support
topic.description: This cluster of documents discusses various legislative acts that involve federal designations, support for specific programs, and appropriations for designated entities and initiatives.

Document 1: Designates Albania, Croatia, Georgia, Macedonia (FYROM), and Ukraine as eligible to receive assistance under the NATO Participation Act of 1994.
Authorizes FY2008 appropriations for security assistance to: (1) Albania; (2) Croatia; (3) Georgia; (4) Macedonia (FYROM); and (5) Ukraine.
Document 2: Amends the Stevenson-Wydler Technology Innovation Act of 1980 to expand the duties and reporting requirements of the Office of Innovation and Entrepreneurship.
authorize appropriations for such Office for FY2014-FY2018.
Document 3: Amends the Pittman-Robertson Wildlife Restoration Act to authorize a state to pay up to 90% of the costs of acquiring land for, expanding, or constructing a public target range.
Urges the Chief of the Forest Service and the Director of the Bureau of Land Management (BLM) to cooperate with state and local authorities and other entities.
Document 4: This bill designates the federal building and U.S. courthouse located at 121 Spring Street S.E. in Gainesville, Georgia, as the 'Sidney Olsin Smith, Jr. Federal Building and United States Courthouse.'
Document 5: Designates the facility of the United States Postal Service located at 12877 Broad Street in Sparta, Georgia, as the 'Yvonne Ingram-Ephraim Post Office Building.'
Document 6: Amends the Reclamation Project Act of 1939 to authorize the Secretary of the Interior to contract for the development of small conduit hydropower at Bureau facilities.
Defines 'small conduit hydropower' as five megawatts or less.

topic.name: Railroad Safety and Community Impact
topic.description: Legislation addressing the safety and environmental effects of railroad operations on local communities.

Document 1: Revises requirements with respect to the consolidation, merger, or acquisition of control involving rail carriers to require public hearings in the affected communities.
Prohibits the Board from approving mergers if their adverse impacts on safety and on affected communities outweigh their transportation benefits.
Document 2: Requires railroad carriers found at fault for an unintended release of hazardous materials to review periodically any post-accident public health assessments.
Directs the Secretary of Transportation to prescribe regulations requiring railroad carriers transporting hazmat to give first responders accurate and current commodity flow data.

topic.name: Federal Research and Development Legislation
topic.description: Legislation related to federal funding and strategic initiatives for research and development in science, technology, and energy sectors.

Document 1: Directs specified federal agencies participating in the National High-Performance Computing Program to transmit to Congress a cybersecurity strategic research and development plan.

Expands permitted National Science Foundation (NSF) grants for basic research on innovative approaches to the structure of computer and network hardware and software that are aimed at enhancing computer security.
Document 3: Authorizes appropriations for FY2011-FY2015 to the National Science Foundation (NSF) for research and related activities.

Directs NSF to implement a policy for a specified broader impacts review criterion, including goals to achieve increased U.S. economic competitiveness, development of a globally competitive STEM workforce, and increased national security.
Document 4: Establishes a national forensic science research program to improve, expand, and coordinate federal research in forensic sciences.

Requires the Director of the National Science Foundation (NSF) to enter into an arrangement with the National Academies to develop a report identifying the most critical forensic science disciplines that require further research.

topic.name: Health and Retirement Legislation
topic.description: This cluster focuses on legislative acts related to health care flexibility, retirement savings, and benefits for specific groups.

Document 1: Repeals provisions of the Patient Protection and Affordable Care Act
allows payments for over-the-counter medications
Document 2: establish a tax-exempt individual retirement account for taxpayers under age 26
income-based tax deduction for contributions
Document 3: authorize and establish requirements for state-sponsored multiple employer retirement plans
state-managed payroll deduction savings programs
Document 4: require the Pension Benefit Guaranty Corporation to compute the actuarial value of monthly benefits
relates to retirement age for commercial airline pilots
Document 5: repeal provisions added by the Patient Protection and Affordable Care Act
impose a $2,500 limitation on salary reduction contributions
Document 6: require a group health plan to continue treating individuals as dependent children until age 25
amends the Employee Retirement Income Security Act
Document 7: exclude Members of Congress from further retirement coverage
affect the eligibility of a Member to participate in the Thrift Savings Plan

topic.name: Small Business Support Legislation
topic.description: This cluster of documents focuses on various legislative amendments aimed at enhancing support, opportunities, and resources for small businesses, particularly those owned by disadvantaged groups and women.

Document 1: Amends the Small Business Act to consider an individual with a net worth of less than $1.5 million as eligible for participation in a Small Business Administration (SBA) program.
Increases from: (1) 23% to 25% the government-wide small business procurement contract goal.
Document 2: Amends the Small Business Act relating to the women's procurement program to require the Administrator of the SBA to wait until five years after the program is implemented before conducting a required study.
States that a contracting officer need not make a finding of past gender discrimination by a contracting agency in order to comply with federal contracting requirements.
Document 3: Amends the Small Business Act with respect to obtaining the best available collateral for a disaster loan.
Expresses the sense of Congress that the Administrator should reduce paperwork burdens on small businesses applying for SBA disaster assistance loans.
Document 4: Amends the Small Business Act to direct each federal agency that is required to establish a small business innovation research (SBIR) program to issue research topic solicitations at least twice a year.
Requires each federal agency conducting an SBIR program to engage with such awardees and develop performance measures for awardee progression.
Document 5: Amends the Small Business Act to direct the SBA to make publicly available a user-friendly database of information relating to lenders making loans under such Act.
Prohibits the SBA from requiring lenders to submit additional information or to otherwise increase burdens on lenders.
Document 6: Amends the Small Business Act to reauthorize through FY2018 the Small Business Innovation Research (SBIR) and Small Business Technology Transfer (STTR) programs.
Increases, for both the SBIR and STTR programs, the individual small business award levels.
Document 7: This bill amends the Small Business Act to reauthorize for FY2017-FY2022 both the Small Business Innovation Research (SBIR) Program and the Small Business Technology Transfer (STTR) Program.
The SBA shall continue through FY2022 to permit each federal agency to use up to 3% of its SBIR program funds to assist the agency in its required SBIR or STTR program's administration.

topic.name: Indian Tribal Sovereignty and Support
topic.description: This topic encompasses legislation and initiatives aimed at supporting the rights, education, and welfare of federally recognized Indian tribes in the United States.

Document 1: loan forgiveness to borrowers who are members of an Indian tribe
employed as a full-time teacher in an Indian school
Document 2: authorizes specific Indian tribes to obtain 99-year lease authority for trust land
Document 3: defines state homes for veterans established by federally recognized Indian tribes
requires VA to pay tribes for care provided to veterans
Document 4: waives application of Indian Self Determination Act to specified real property
prohibits gaming under the Indian Gaming Regulatory Act
Document 5: loan forgiveness to borrowers who are members of an Indian tribe
employed as a full-time teacher in an Indian school
Document 6: extends exemption from prohibitions on transportation of explosive materials to Indian tribes
Document 7: establishes Office of Tribal Government Homeland Security
requires Secretary to treat Indian tribes as states for homeland security purposes

topic.name: Social Security and Disability Benefits

Treats a violation of this Act as an unfair or deceptive act or practice under the Federal Trade Commission Act.
